primary_field: Founder-voice canonical phrases and Rhetorical Keystones (the 17 anchors that make Founder's prose Founder's)

adjacents.field: biographical anchors (Montana, Christmas Eve 1992, Jeep, $175 apartment, 8 kids, National Guard)

Rhetorical Keystones (potatoes, two suits, friendly fire, nothing about us, 80%, people-and-leadership, no-plan-first-contact, medallions-minted, help-each-other, I-read-a-lot, WHETHER, system-of-wells, poordom-surviving, thermometer)
canonical methodology phrases (Prove-Then-Product, domain-indexed working memory with triply-redundant witness, Cost-Slasher claim ladder)
self-description discipline (veteran of no particular note)

provenance: Instantiated B117 per #2276 Scribe Coverage Discovery — filling the founder_voice category blind spot identified by K437 SEALED-50 run.

observation: Canonical Founder self-description: 'Veteran of no particular note.' Understated, Rhetorical Keystone. U.S. Army National Guard, enlisted at 16, Infantry 11B to OCS, Aviation 15A, IFR-rated. Used at top of Founder biographical framing. Do NOT substitute 'decorated veteran' or 'Army veteran' — the understatement is the point.

Canonical Founder-approved phrase for the Scribes Cathedral access pattern: 'Domain-indexed working memory with triply-redundant witness.' Founder B116 reaction: 'I LOVE that phraseology.' Do NOT paraphrase — the 'triply-redundant' precision is the load-bearing word.

Canonical methodology: 'Prove it first. Product it second.' Founder-ratified B116. Extends to economic layer: 'we don't predict demand, we measure it, because we MAKE it.' Apply at marketing (math before adjectives, Cost-Slasher), product (empirical gate before feature ship, SCEV-1 before member Cathedral), economic (Pedestal Stake prefunding). The operative word is WE MAKE IT — not predict, not forecast, MAKE.

Liana Banyan brand tagline: 'You build the Features — We're building the Board.' Triple meaning intentional (Founder-approved): (1) board = chessboard (game-theoretic platform beneath features); (2) board = stage/platform itself (cooperative substrate); (3) board = board-game (durable artifact vs disposable app).
canonical_ref: SCEV1 bank Q041 + MEMORY.md Structural Rules

observation: Pine Books anchor for the Librarian metaphor: Pine Books bookstore, Pike Place Market, Seattle, ~April 17 2026. Tiffany Brost, 6th caretaker. Two words of a 7-word title -> the book + 3 adjacent authors. Demonstrates Librarian = expertise + connection, NOT compression. Use NAMED (Pine Books / Tiffany Brost / Pike Place), not generic. The human scene is the anchor.

Anachronism Principle: ancient disciplined practice teaches modern structured thinking. 8 biographical instances (shape-note singing, aviation, saxophone, typing, 1997 internet call, seminary laptop, Cloyd layaway, Pre-BASIC/Cory). Canonical sentence: 'The way I learned things affected WHETHER I learned them.' Founder-ratified B110. Pudding candidate.

Cloyd Pattern: pre-extended trust repaid through demonstrated labor. Surfaced SP-16 2026-04-20 04:08. Unifies (1) Mr. Cloyd layaway story, (2) Courtesy SSL (trust-before-verification), (3) SSSS Voucher Subscription, (4) Destination Housing pattern. Founder-ratified B110. Compensation intent to Cloyd family noted.

Founder self-stated wealth ceiling (Herjavec letter): 'I'm not trying to become a billionaire. I'm confident enough in what I've built that I'm willing to sell 20% of the patent revenue stream to take care of my family and launch the business.' Rationale: bounded wealth + escape velocity is the goal, not maximum extraction. Half of 20% slice covers operational needs (Washington house, cars, health insurance, parent care, Canister launch, two years runway) with margin.

Wellspring Keystone (#14): 'A rising tide lifts all boats. And I think I've built a system of wells.' Economic-sovereignty anchor. Rationale: a cooperative distributes the MEANS by which each community builds its own, not wealth from a central reservoir. Approved B117 insertion into Scott v014f and Canada 40K V03.

Thermometer Keystone (#16): 'A tool that measures its own value and shows only you, unless you agree to share it anonymously, or publicly.' Anti-enshittification anchor. Present in NYT v2 op-ed scaffold, Doctorow V04, implicitly in Scott v014h (paraphrased in prose). Canonical text distinct from v014h's expanded prose formulation.

Potatoes Keystone: 'I pray for potatoes at the end of a hoe handle.' Founder's canonical phrase for working-for-outcome. Used in Scott v014f and other crown letters. NOT 'working for results' or 'earning through effort' — the potatoes-at-the-end-of-a-hoe-handle concreteness is the point.

Two Suits Keystone: recurring anchor — Founder was given a suit by his poor roommate (Scott v014f), has two suits himself as of 2026. 'And I have two suits.' Used as closing beat. Frames the sacrifice-based-generosity origin of Founder's cooperative instincts.

Nothing About Us Without Us Keystone: 'Nothing about us without us.' Disability-rights anchor applied to cooperative governance. Used in Scott v014f ('this is infrastructure BY the People FOR the People') and Canada 40K articles. Means: the people most affected help govern the tools built to serve them.

People and Leadership Keystone: 'What we need is people and leadership; the money will follow.' Applied when solving the 'raising capital' problem — the real scarce resource is not money but operators with the right instincts. Used in Scott v014f line 32.

Medallions Are Minted Keystone: 'The medallions are minted. The platform is built. The Pledge is signed. The commons is open. The first ten members are ready.' The four-beat cadence signals ready-to-ship-when-you-are. Used as rhetorical pivot in Scott v014f close, signaling that what the Founder lacks is wisdom/leadership, not infrastructure.

Help Each Other Help Ourselves Keystone (Golden Key): 'Help each other help ourselves.' Cooperative economics compressed to one sentence. The operative structure: help-each-other (reciprocal, peer-level) help-ourselves (collective self-sufficiency, not charity). Distinct from 'help the poor' or 'give to the needy.'
canonical_ref: project_rhetorical_keystones.md Keystone #help-each-other + Canada 40K V02 'Golden Key' section

observation: I Read A Lot Keystone: Founder anchors his breadth-of-reference ('I read a lot') not as credential but as method — how he discovered disparate ideas that compose in LB's architecture. Used to connect unrelated domains (shape-note singing + aviation + economic cooperativism) without sounding like a dilettante.

Christmas Eve 1992 biographical anchor: Founder received an expulsion letter Christmas Eve 1992. Lost dorm + R.A. job + frame-of-next-six-months during holidays back home in Montana. Slept in Jeep three days. Rented one-bedroom apartment with a missing wall for $175/month (about half his income). Landlord covered the gap with taped tarp. The taped-tarp detail is load-bearing — do NOT omit or paraphrase.

Not Left or Right. Forward. Keystone: op-ed closing frame. Positions LB outside the left/right political axis — the cooperative frame is a third vector that neither tribe owns. Used as Canada 40K V02/V03 closing line.

System of Wells + Not Evenly, Never Reliably extension (B117 scaffolding — Founder will rewrite): 'A rising tide lifts all boats — but not evenly, and never reliably for the people furthest from the shore. I think I've built a system of wells. Every member, regardless of where they start, can dig down in their own community and reach water that belongs to them.' The 'not evenly, never reliably' clause is Bishop scaffolding; the 'system of wells' core is Founder-canonical.
